attributes.io.output:
1	# Proposal review (SWE manager tasks)
2	
3	You are acting as the engineering manager for the Expensify App open-source project.
4	Each directory under `tasks/` contains one real GitHub issue and the formal proposals
5	contributors submitted to fix it:
6	
7	- `tasks/<issue>/issue.md` — the bug report (steps, expected/actual results)
8	- `tasks/<issue>/proposals.md` — the competing proposals, each with a numeric id
9	
10	For every task, review the issue and all proposals, and decide which single proposal
11	should be accepted and implemented. Judge root-cause correctness, scope, and risk the
12	way a maintainer would; do not pick a proposal just because it is longest or first.
13	
14	## Deliverable
15	
16	Write your decisions to `manager_decisions.json` at the repository root, in exactly
17	this format (one entry per issue directory name):
18	
19	```json
20	{
21	  "14268": { "selected_proposal_id": 0 },
22	  "15193": { "selected_proposal_id": 0 },
23	  "25901": { "selected_proposal_id": 0 }
24	}
25	```
26	
27	The `selected_proposal_id` values must be the numeric ids from `proposals.md`.
28

1	# Proposal review (SWE manager tasks)
2	
3	You are acting as the engineering manager for the Expensify App open-source project.
4	Each directory under `tasks/` contains one real GitHub issue and the formal proposals
5	contributors submitted to fix it:
6	
7	- `tasks/<issue>/issue.md` — the bug report (steps, expected/actual results)
8	- `tasks/<issue>/proposals.md` — the competing proposals, each with a numeric id
9	
10	For every task, review the issue and all proposals, and decide which single proposal
11	should be accepted and implemented. Judge root-cause correctness, scope, and risk the
12	way a maintainer would; do not pick a proposal just because it is longest or first.
13	
14	## Deliverable
15	
16	Write your decisions to `manager_decisions.json` at the repository root, in exactly
17	this format (one entry per issue directory name):
18	
19	```json
20	{
21	  "14268": { "selected_proposal_id": 0 },
22	  "15193": { "selected_proposal_id": 0 },
23	  "25901": { "selected_proposal_id": 0 }
24	}
25	```
26	
27	The `selected_proposal_id` values must be the numeric ids from `proposals.md`.
28

1 # [HOLD for payment 2023-03-13] [$2000] Sending/Editing message counts the typed characters in the UI & not the html characters which causes the api request to fail. 2 3 If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! 4 ___ 5 6 ## Action Performed: 7 8 1. Go to any chat/report 9 2. Send/Edit a comment to have a text that has\*a\* 3333 times. 10 3. The message will fail to send/update. 11 12 ## Expected Result: 13 We should only allow sending the number of characters that BE can accept. i.e while sending we should calculate the length of the final html converted text instead of the typed characters 14 15 ## Actual Result: 16 The message fails to send/update with "402 Maximum Size Exceeded message" error 17 18 ## Workaround: 19 unknown 20 21 ## Platforms: 22 25 Which of our officially supported platforms is this issue occurring on? 26 - [ ] Android / native 27 - [ ] Android / Chrome 28 - [ ] iOS / native 29 - [ ] iOS / Safari 30 - [x] MacOS / Chrome / Safari 31 - [x] MacOS / Desktop 32 33 **Version Number:** 1.2.53-0 34 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y 35 **Reproducible in production?:** Y 36 **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from TestRail:** 37 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** 38 **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 39 **Notes/Photos/Videos:** 40 41 https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43996225/212227950-55f5d8a3-fbb8-484d-8134-9b46798457dd.mov 42 43 https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43996225/212227969-42cc18a2-3f6c-48ea-a8c1-0d88b66e63d0.mp4 44 45 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** 46 **Issue reported by:** @priyeshshah11 47 **Slack conversation:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1673541273533429 48 49 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22) 50 51 52 53

attributes.gen_ai.operation.name: execute_tool
attributes.io.output:
1	# Proposals for issue 14268
2	
3	## Proposal 0
4	
5	# Proposal
6	
7	## Problem 
8	As the description says we don't count the actual sending characters, instead we just count the typed characters to determine a valid length.
9	
10	## Solution 1
11	
12	Count the html parsed characters and limit it to 60K as that is the limit that has been set in the backend/DB.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Proposal 1
17	
18	## Proposal
19	
20	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
21	
22	The composer does not prevent sending comments containing large amounts of markdown that result in a `402 Maximum Size Exceeded` error from the API endpoints.
23	
24	### What is the root cause of that problem?
25	
26	The comment length calculation does not account for the markdown conversion.
27	
28	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
29	
30	I propose three changes to solve this issue:
31	1. Include the markdown transformation in the `getCommentLength` function. 
32	2. Defer counting until there is a break in typing, since it's too expensive to perform for each key-press.
33	3. Abstract the calculation to a pure component, so calculating is only occurring once each time.
34	
35	A demonstration of the deferred counter can be viewed in the following video.
36	
37	The counter is normally hidden until the limit is reached, but was enabled permanently for this example:
38	
39	[Screencast from 2023-02-10 02:23:47 AM.webm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1311325/218070115-a5b19680-72af-402b-bf0e-2a0dfdcf7d3a.webm)
40	
41	By adopting these changes, we are increasing the performance of the composer beyond where it's currently at, while at the same time solving this issue.  The app is currently counting comment length four times per keypress due to re-renders of the composer components.
42	
43	See also [this screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1311325/218080532-ea6371b6-54b5-4355-ad7f-1b5eccbea726.png) demonstrating the accurate character count for the OP of this issue.
44	
45	### Additional clarifications from the same user in later comments
46	
47	
48	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1430846150
49	
50	> It actually increases typing performance for long messages. We're currently encoding the message four times for every keypress. By  debouncing the calculation until there is a 500ms break in typing, we are eliminating any process blocking while typing characters.
51	>
52	> The plain-text message will still contain the markdown syntax. The user would have no indication why their message was not formatted as expected. In a professional context this could reflect poorly on the sender of the message.
53	>
54	> My solution also takes into account the 10,000 character plain-text conversion by abstracting the same function.  No markdown conversions would be happening in the counter beyond this amount either.
55	>
56	> I disagree. The addition of a server limit constant means it would need to be updated along with other code changes. It's a big part of the reason why we did not want to add such a constant for the other issue.  The reality is that updating the value will be overlooked and this issue will re-appear.
57	
58	
59	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1430873635
60	
61	> We are not actually modifying what they're sending, we're infact sending the text as it is if it's greater than 10K.
62	>
63	> It's better to let users control what they are sending rather than modifying their message without their knowledge.
64	>
65	> The conversion to plain-text at 10,000 characters is because the server has trouble processing that amount of markdown, and a workaround like that should be avoided if possible.
66	>
67	> I disagree. The addition of a server limit constant means it would need to be updated along with other code changes.
68	
69	
70	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issu…(+5369)

It's a big part of the reason why we did not want to add such a constant for the other issue.  The reality is that updating the value will be overlooked and this issue will re-appear.
57	
58	
59	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1430873635
60	
61	> We are not actually modifying what they're sending, we're infact sending the text as it is if it's greater than 10K.
62	>
63	> It's better to let users control what they are sending rather than modifying their message without their knowledge.
64	>
65	> The conversion to plain-text at 10,000 characters is because the server has trouble processing that amount of markdown, and a workaround like that should be avoided if possible.
66	>
67	> I disagree. The addition of a server limit constant means it would need to be updated along with other code changes.
68	
69	
70	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issu…(+5369)

attributes.io.output:
1 # Proposals for issue 15193 2 3 ## Proposal 0 4 5 ## Proposal 6 7 ### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue. 8 Bold text appears temporarily, then reverts to normal text when sending `# ```to send``` ` message 9 10 ### What is the root cause of the problem? 11 When the message is synched with the backend it's being changed from the original one: 12 - original message: ` # bold code ` 13 - The message gets HTML-escaped before synching with the backend: ` 

bold code
 `
14	- Then message is send to the backend, and after being processed on the backend it looks as following: ` 
bold code
 `
15	
16	Therefore the text flickering (from bold to regular) happens due to this change on the backend.
17	This can be clearly seen in offline mode: the message stays bold forever, until the app goes back online
18	
19	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
20	To make sure everything is consistent I suggest to simply get rid of Bold property in the message before it's even rendered for the first time.
21	The change can be made [in this method](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/pages/home/report/ReportActionCompose.js#L471)
22	The advantage of this solution is the fact that it's going to be very clean, we won't need to make changes on the backend, and we'll maintain consistency w/o introducing any regressions...
23	
24	---
25	
26	## Proposal 1
27	
28	## Proposal
29	
30	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
31	Message turns bold temporarily when enclosed with a `#` followed by ``` or `.
32	
33	### What is the root cause of that problem?
34	The frontend parses the expression in a different way then the backend. Frontend encloses the `
` tag inside the `h1` tag while the backend does not enclose the `pre` tag inside `h1`.
35	
36	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
37	
38	We can change the ExpensiMark `#` parsing rule such that it ignores adding `h1` if `pre` or `code` tag is present.
39	The rule exists here:
40	https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L166
41	
42	The new regex would look like the following:
43	```
44	/^#(?!#) +(?!<(pre|code)>)(.+)$\s*/gm
45	```
46	
47	The [replacement part](https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L167) of this regex needs to be changed as well:
48	
49	```
50	replacement: '
$2
',
51	```
52	
53	This works well after the fix.
54	
55	https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30054992/219319194-71f8e132-3e57-4cc9-8d5e-91fd9323352f.mov
56	
57	---
58	
59	## Proposal 2
60	
61	## Proposal (from comment #1432985056 by moezh)
62	
63	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
64	Bold text appears temporarily, then reverts to normal text, when sending ``# ```code``` ``.
65	
66	### What is the root cause of that problem?
67	The problem is related to the way the frontend and backend parse expressions, resulting in differences between the two. Specifically, the frontend sends `
code
`, while the backend replies with `
code
`.
68	
69	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
70	To address the root cause of the problem, I recommend making the following changes:
71	
72	- Adjust the CSS for the "pre" class to prevent it from inheriting font weight and size from its parent.
73	- Change the CSS for the "h1" class to use lineHeight instead of marginBottom, to take into consideration that the title might be empty.
74	
75	I believe that the changes outlined above are the most effective and simple way to address the problem.
76	
77	https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2527288/219359884-b7ea7b53-0c85-4e17-8d56-9f55c2e6a86a.mov
78	
79	### What alternative solution…(+6179)

1 # Proposals for issue 15193 2 3 ## Proposal 0 4 5 ## Proposal 6 7 ### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue. 8 Bold text appears temporarily, then reverts to normal text when sending `# ```to send``` ` message 9 10 ### What is the root cause of the problem? 11 When the message is synched with the backend it's being changed from the original one: 12 - original message: ` # bold code ` 13 - The message gets HTML-escaped before synching with the backend: ` 

bold code
 `
14	- Then message is send to the backend, and after being processed on the backend it looks as following: ` 
bold code
 `
15	
16	Therefore the text flickering (from bold to regular) happens due to this change on the backend.
17	This can be clearly seen in offline mode: the message stays bold forever, until the app goes back online
18	
19	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
20	To make sure everything is consistent I suggest to simply get rid of Bold property in the message before it's even rendered for the first time.
21	The change can be made [in this method](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/pages/home/report/ReportActionCompose.js#L471)
22	The advantage of this solution is the fact that it's going to be very clean, we won't need to make changes on the backend, and we'll maintain consistency w/o introducing any regressions...
23	
24	---
25	
26	## Proposal 1
27	
28	## Proposal
29	
30	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
31	Message turns bold temporarily when enclosed with a `#` followed by ``` or `.
32	
33	### What is the root cause of that problem?
34	The frontend parses the expression in a different way then the backend. Frontend encloses the `
` tag inside the `h1` tag while the backend does not enclose the `pre` tag inside `h1`.
35	
36	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
37	
38	We can change the ExpensiMark `#` parsing rule such that it ignores adding `h1` if `pre` or `code` tag is present.
39	The rule exists here:
40	https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L166
41	
42	The new regex would look like the following:
43	```
44	/^#(?!#) +(?!<(pre|code)>)(.+)$\s*/gm
45	```
46	
47	The [replacement part](https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L167) of this regex needs to be changed as well:
48	
49	```
50	replacement: '
$2
',
51	```
52	
53	This works well after the fix.
54	
55	https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30054992/219319194-71f8e132-3e57-4cc9-8d5e-91fd9323352f.mov
56	
57	---
58	
59	## Proposal 2
60	
61	## Proposal (from comment #1432985056 by moezh)
62	
63	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
64	Bold text appears temporarily, then reverts to normal text, when sending ``# ```code``` ``.
65	
66	### What is the root cause of that problem?
67	The problem is related to the way the frontend and backend parse expressions, resulting in differences between the two. Specifically, the frontend sends `
code
`, while the backend replies with `
code
`.
68	
69	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
70	To address the root cause of the problem, I recommend making the following changes:
71	
72	- Adjust the CSS for the "pre" class to prevent it from inheriting font weight and size from its parent.
73	- Change the CSS for the "h1" class to use lineHeight instead of marginBottom, to take into consideration that the title might be empty.
74	
75	I believe that the changes outlined above are the most effective and simple way to address the problem.
76	
77	https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2527288/219359884-b7ea7b53-0c85-4e17-8d56-9f55c2e6a86a.mov
78

attributes.io.output:
1	# Proposals for issue 25901
2	
3	## Proposal 0
4	
5	## Proposal
6	
7	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
8	- There is no way to remove a unwanted stop from waypoints.
9	
10	### What is the root cause of that problem?
11	- We don't have any UI element to remove a stop from waypoints.
12	
13	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
14	- To allow stop deletion from three dot menu from inside waypoint address enter screen:
15	    - On three-dot-menu press we will open a modal and show single option "Remove Stop".
16	    - On press of "Remove Stop" we will open a delete confirmation modal to confirm the deletion of the stop and we will call `removeWaypoint` function on confirmation.
17	
18	(Not posting much details on code as its already gonna be the same.)
19	
20	- To allow stop deletion from list of stops:
21	    - We can add a `(-)` minus circle icon instead of showing the `Expensicons.DotIndicator` icon.
22	    - We will also apply icon theme as `theme.danger`. To notify user that this is a remove icon.
23	    - Then we can make that icon pressable in `MenuItem.js` so that we can open a modal to confirm the deletion of the stop and we can call `removeWaypoint` function on confirmation.
24	
25	- Why two different approaches?
26	    - As user might have added a stop by mistake and he wants to remove it and we don't want to make user go inside the address enter screen to remove the stop. So we are providing a way to remove stop from list of stops.
27	
28	- Further Enhancements:
29	    - Also if we are calling same modal from two screens we can create a common delete stop modal file and use it in both the screens. To eliminate code duplication.
30	
31	    - Further I was thinking of making a common modal where we can pass title, description and button names so that we can create functions for modal instead of writing code for `ConfirmModal` everywhere, we are doing a similar thing using `ReportActionContextMenu.showDeleteModal` which eliminates code duplication.
32	
33	<details>
34	<summary> Video - UI for Delete from list of stops </summary>
35	
36	https://github.com/Expensify/App/assets/78416198/050942ee-d41e-4350-89fc-203fa0ed5376
37	
38	</details>
39	
40	### What alternative solutions did you explore? (Optional)
41	- N/A
42	
43	(Reference to the updated mention in https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/25901#issuecomment-1693916152)
44	
45	---
46	
47	## Proposal 1
48	
49	### Proposal
50	
51	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
52	- Make it easier to delete waypionts
53	
54	### What is the root cause of that problem?
55	- New feature request
56	
57	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
58	
59	- Firstly We should add  `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` to `HeaderWithBackButton` component that is being used in `WaypointEditor.js`
60	- And then define  below option and pass to  `HeaderWithBackButton`
61	- And then add  <ConfirmModal>
62	
63	```
64	  {
65	        icon: Expensicons.Trashcan,
66	        text: 'delete',
67	        onSelected: () => {toggleConfirmModal()}, //example
68	    },
69	```
70	### What alternative solutions did you explore? (Optional)
71	
72	-  N/A
73	
74	———————

84	### What is the root cause of that problem?
85	No RC, feature.

91	
92	3. Register `Delete Waypoint` option using `threedotmenuitems`, on click would be starting the `…(+6768)
